content:
Part Number Allocation
Governance · Number Families · Assignment Lifecycle
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Anyone introducing a new part, assembly, software artifact, or service item to ERPNext. The Part Number Allocation process must be completed before any new Item record can be saved. This process is owned by Engineering User, InductOne Process Architect, or System Manager (Michael King). --- ## What Part Number Allocation Is Every item in ERPNext must have a **Part Number Assignment** record before it can be created. This is enforced at the Item save level — ERPNext will block the save with an error if no matching Assignment exists. The Assignment is not just a formality. It establishes: - That the number was deliberately reserved, not accidentally typed - Which number family it belongs to (and therefore which series it draws from) - The GitLab Engineering Change that authorized its creation - A permanent audit trail of who requested it, when, and why Numbers are allocated in bulk through a **Part Number Allocation Request** — a formal intake record that generates multiple Assignment records in a single operation. This is the correct path for any new Engineering Change that introduces multiple parts at once. --- ## Number Families and Series Plus One Robotics uses four number families, each drawing from a dedicated numeric series:
Family Series Used For
Part 1xxxxxx Purchased parts, raw materials, discrete components — anything that is bought, not built
Assembly 2xxxxxx Built assemblies with a BOM — sub-assemblies, top-level machine assemblies, fabricated items
Software 3xxxxxx Software artifacts, firmware packages, and other non-physical deliverables tracked in ERPNext
Service 4xxxxxx Service items — labor, support contracts, field services, and other non-stocked service line items
> ⚠️ **The number family determines the series permanently.** A Part number (`1xxxxxx`) cannot be reclassified as an Assembly (`2xxxxxx`) after allocation. If the wrong family was selected, contact Operations — correction requires a new allocation and a retired Assignment on the incorrect one. **Custom numbers** cannot be auto-allocated through the Allocation Request. If a specific number is required for a legacy part or special circumstance, Part Number Assignment records must be created manually by the Engineering User. See the Legacy Parts section below. --- ## The Part Number Assignment Lifecycle A Part Number Assignment moves through the lifecycle defined by the allocation workflow: | Status | Meaning | |---|---| | `Reserved` | A number has been allocated but the engineering record is not yet released. | | `In Development` | The number is actively being used for engineering development. | | `Released` | The related Item/BOM definition is released for normal use. | | `Cancelled` | The allocation was abandoned and is retained for audit. | | `Superseded` | The allocation was replaced by a later controlled definition. | The important distinction is that allocation reserves a number; it does not by itself mean the engineering content is released. --- ## Standard Process — New Engineering Change Use this path for any new Engineering Change that introduces new parts. This is the correct process for all new introductions going forward. ### Step 1 — Open a Part Number Allocation Request Navigate to: Part Number Allocation Request list → **New** Fill in the header: - **Requestor** — your name - **GitLab EC URL** — the URL of the GitLab Engineering Change that covers these parts. This is required and will be copied to each Assignment record created - **GitLab Reference** — the MR or tag reference if applicable - **Notes** — describe the context for this allocation (e.g. "New gripper variant for InductOne LH build") ### Step 2 — Add Requested Numbers Rows In the **Requested Numbers** child table, add one row per number family you need numbers for: | Field | What to Enter | |---|---| | `number_family` | Part, Assembly, Software, or Service | | `quantity_requested` | How many numbers you need from that family | | `description_requested` | Brief description of what this family of numbers is for | The **Total Numbers Requested** field updates automatically as you add rows. > 💡 You can request multiple families in a single Allocation Request. If an EC introduces 3 purchased parts and 2 assemblies, add two rows — one for Part qty 3 and one for Assembly qty 2. ### Step 3 — Allocate Numbers Save the request. Click **Allocate Numbers** (primary blue button). The confirmation dialog warns: *"This will permanently burn the generated numbers and create Part Number Assignment records."* Confirm to proceed. The system calls `inductone_tools.part_numbering.allocate_numbers` server-side, which: 1. Draws the next available numbers from each requested family's series 2. Creates a `Part Number Assignment` record for each number at status `Reserved` 3. Stamps the GitLab EC URL from the request onto each Assignment 4. Sets the Allocation Request status to `Allocated` 5. Returns a summary listing every number created The summary popup shows each allocated number and its family. **Screenshot or note these numbers** — they are the item codes you will use when creating Items in the next step. ### Step 4 — Create Items With Assignments now existing at `Reserved` status, proceed to create Item records for each allocated number. See [Creating a New Item (Non-Assembly)](plus-one-ops-manual/creating-a-new-item) for the full Item creation procedure. When you save each Item: - Link `custom_part_number_assignment` to the correct Assignment record - The `custom_gitlab_ec_url` field will auto-populate from the linked Assignment - On save, the Assignment automatically transitions to `Released` ### Step 5 — Create BOMs and Engineering Signoff (Assemblies only) For every `2xxxxxx` Assembly item: 1. Create the BOM, add all component items, save, then submit 2. Saving the new BOM record triggers the Engineering Signoff `after_insert` hook — a Pending signoff record is created automatically 3. A user with the **Engineering User** role reviews and approves the BOM The Assembly is not usable in production or InductOne builds until its BOM has an approved signoff. See [Engineering Signoff](plus-one-ops-manual/engineering-signoff) for the full approval workflow. --- ## Legacy Parts Legacy parts are parts that predate the allocation process or were intentionally created through manual/Custom assignment. Do not infer legacy status from a numeric threshold. The current model uses a shared global sequence with family prefixes, so legitimate Part numbers can have suffixes that look numerically lower than older Assembly numbers. When a legacy/manual item must be brought under control, document the assignment history rather than renumbering live engineering records. --- ## What Blocks a New Item Save If you attempt to save a new Item and see the error:
Item Code [XXXXXXX] is controlled. Select the matching Part Number Assignment before saving.
This means no `Part Number Assignment` record exists for this item code. Do not attempt to work around this error. The correct resolution is: 1. If this is a new part from an active EC — go back and complete the Allocation Request first 2. If this is a legacy part — create the Assignment manually as described above 3. If you are unsure — contact Operations before touching the Item --- ## Part Number Assignment List View all Part Number Assignments — filter by `status = Reserved` to see numbers that have been allocated but do not yet have an Item record. --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Administration & Governance · Part Number Allocation Robots work. People rule.

content:
BOMs & Engineering Signoff
Creating Bills of Materials · Revisions · The Signoff Gate
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Whoever transcribes engineering drawings into ERPNext BOMs — today that is Operations. Engineering produces the drawings and the BOM design; Operations builds the corresponding parts and BOMs in ERPNext. This page covers that transcription, the revision mechanic, and the Engineering Signoff gate that governs whether a BOM can be used. > ⚠️ **The order is not optional.** A BOM cannot be built until the item it produces exists, and that item cannot exist until its part number is allocated and linked. Part number → Item → BOM. Do not attempt to shortcut this chain. --- ## Where a BOM sits in the bigger picture A BOM in our system is not just a parts list — it is a controlled, signed-off configuration record. Before it can be used in a build release, it must be **Active**, **Default**, **submitted**, and carry a **current Engineering Signoff**. Miss any one of those and the BOM is not usable, and the release readiness check in the InductOne pipeline will block on it. This page covers how a BOM comes into being and how it earns its signoff. For how BOMs are consumed downstream, see the InductOne Build Pipeline. For finding and reading existing BOMs, see BOM Structure & Search. --- ## Prerequisite — the part must exist first A BOM describes how to build a specific item. That item — the thing the BOM produces — must already exist in ERPNext before the BOM can be created. For a brand-new part that has never existed, the full chain is: 1. **Allocate the part number** — via a Part Number Allocation Request, drawing from the correct number family. See Part Number Allocation. 2. **Create the Item** — linking the allocated Part Number Assignment and the GitLab Engineering Change (EC) URL at creation time. See Creating a New Item. 3. **Then create the BOM** — with that item as the item being produced. > 💡 The part number and EC linkage live on the **Item**, not the BOM. They are established when the item is created and carry forward automatically. You do not re-enter them on the BOM. If you find yourself wanting to type a part number into a BOM, stop — the item it points to probably does not exist yet. > ⚠️ This applies to every new producible item in the tree, not just the top assembly. If a sub-assembly is new, its item must be created (with its own part number and EC link) before a BOM can be written for it. --- ## Creating a new BOM **Navigate to:** New BOM (or BOM list → **+ New**) This is standard ERPNext BOM creation. Build the BOM to match the engineering drawing: | Field | What to Enter | |---|---| | `item` | The item this BOM produces — the item you created in the prerequisite step. Its description and UOM auto-populate | | `quantity` | The quantity this BOM yields (normally 1) | | `is_active` | Checked — the BOM is in use | | `is_default` | Checked — this is the default BOM for the item (see revision rules below) | | Items table | Each component: child item code, quantity, and UOM. Sub-assemblies reference their own BOMs | Work down the drawing and add every component to the items table. For sub-assemblies that have their own BOMs, ERPNext links them so the tree explodes correctly downstream. **When the BOM matches the drawing:** 1. **Save** the BOM. ERPNext assigns the name `BOM-{item_code}-001` for the first BOM of this item (the `-001` revision token). 2. On save, a **Pending Engineering Signoff is created automatically** and a yellow banner appears on the form (see the Signoff section below). 3. **Submit** the BOM (`docstatus = 1`) when it is complete and correct. A submitted BOM is immutable — this is intentional and is the basis of the revision mechanic. > ⚠️ Electrical BOMs carry `ELEC` in the BOM name and have additional balloon-callout fields on their item rows (balloon number, electrical unit, source revision). These feed the Electrical Balloon Callouts report. See BOM Structure & Search for that report. --- ## Revising a BOM — there is no editing A submitted BOM is immutable. You never edit an existing BOM to change it. To revise, you create a **new version**, and our convention strictly governs which version is live. **From the existing BOM, use the New Version button.** This is ERPNext's native BOM versioning — it carries the current items forward into a new BOM record so you are not rebuilding from scratch. **Then:** 1. **Increment the revision token manually** to match the format `BOM-{item_code}-00n` — e.g. `-001` becomes `-002`. Set this by hand to keep the revision sequence correct and legible. 2. Make the changes that prompted the revision. 3. **Save** — a fresh Pending Engineering Signoff is created for this new version (it is a new BOM record, so it needs its own review — the prior version's approval does not carry over). 4. **Submit** the new version. 5. **Set the new version as Default and Active.** Only one BOM per item can be Default. 6. **Deactivate the prior version** — uncheck `is_active` on the old BOM. > ⚠️ **Strictly enforced rule: most recent = Default + Active. All prior versions = inactive.** This is non-negotiable. Setting the new version as default does not automatically clean up the old one to our standard — you must turn off the prior version yourself. Two active BOMs for one item is an error state that will cause the wrong BOM to be pulled into builds. > 💡 Because each revision is a separate BOM record, the full revision history is preserved — old versions remain in the system as inactive records, and any snapshot that was frozen against an old revision still references exactly what it was built with. --- ## Engineering Signoff — the gate Engineering Signoff is a two-pairs-of-eyes governance gate. A BOM (or Product Bundle) must be explicitly reviewed and approved by a designated engineer before it can be released to an external builder. It catches "did Operations transcribe this BOM correctly into ERPNext?" before anything ships. Signoff is **per BOM revision.** Approving `BOM-1611 027 0200-001` does **not** approve a later `-002`. Each revision is a new review. ### Who can sign off Users with the **`Engineering User`**, **`InductOne Process Architect`**, or **`System Manager`** role can approve or reject signoffs. Current Engineering User approvers include: - Shaun Edwards - Jason Minica - Wayne Kirk - David Moreno System Manager authority is reserved for system ownership or delegated process control. > 💡 Operations creates the BOM but cannot approve its own signoff. That separation is the entire point of the gate — the person who built the record in ERPNext is not the person who certifies it is correct. ### The banner — what each state means When you open a BOM, a colored banner near the top shows its signoff state: | Banner | Meaning | |---|---| | ✓ Approved — by [name] at [time] | Engineering has signed off. Approved for use in releases | | ⏳ Pending | Awaiting engineering review. Cannot be released to a builder | | ✗ Rejected | Engineering rejected with notes. Needs fixes and a new version | | (no banner) | No signoff configured — either it predates signoff, or signoff is not required for this record | One more state worth knowing: **"This signoff is no longer current. The target has been modified since approval."** — the record was changed after it was approved, so the approval no longer applies. It needs re-review. (For BOMs this is rare because submitted BOMs are immutable, but it is the governing logic for Product Bundles, which can be edited.) --- ## How the signoff flow works ### When Operations creates or revises a BOM 1. You save the BOM 2. A **Pending Engineering Signoff** is created automatically in the background 3. The BOM shows the yellow **Pending** banner 4. The BOM can be referenced internally — you can keep working, generate snapshots, and so on — but it **cannot be released to a builder** until approved ### When Engineering reviews 1. The engineer opens the BOM (where **Approve** / **Reject** actions appear to Signoff-role users) or the Engineering Signoff record directly 2. They review the BOM against the drawing 3. They click **Approve** (notes optional) or **Reject** (reason required — it becomes part of the permanent audit trail) 4. The banner updates to Green or Red ### When an approved BOM is superseded by a new revision Because revisions are new BOM records, the new version starts fresh at Pending and goes through its own review. The prior version keeps its approval on record (as an inactive BOM), but it is no longer the default and is no longer used. --- ## What signoff blocks > ⚠️ **Release to Builder is blocked unless the controlled release inputs are approved.** The release readiness check covers the top BOM, top Item, Product Bundle when present, and selected Configuration Options. It does not recursively require every child BOM in the configured tree. This is why a single un-approved sub-assembly BOM deep in the tree will stop a release. The fix is never to work around the gate — it is to get that BOM reviewed. --- ## Troubleshooting
The BOM I need won't appear when setting top_bom on a build `top_bom` (and BOM links generally) are filtered to **active, submitted** BOMs. If your BOM doesn't appear, check that it is both `is_active = 1` and `docstatus = 1` (submitted). A saved-but-not-submitted BOM, or a deactivated prior revision, will not show.
I need to fix a mistake in a submitted BOM You cannot edit a submitted BOM — that is by design. Create a new version with the New Version button, increment the revision token, make the fix, submit, set it Default + Active, and deactivate the prior version. The new version will go through its own signoff.
Two BOMs for the same item are both active This is an error state. Our rule is strictly one active/default BOM per item — the most recent revision. Deactivate every prior revision (uncheck `is_active`) so only the current one remains active. Until you do, builds may pull the wrong BOM.
Prepare Handoff fails with an Engineering Signoff error One or more BOMs in the configured tree lack a current Approved signoff. The check walks the entire tree, not just the top BOM. Contact Engineering (Signoff role) with the specific BOM names from the failure output so they can review them. Do not modify the BOMs to work around it.
I created the BOM but the item dropdown is empty / the item doesn't exist The producible item must be created before the BOM. If the item isn't there, you skipped the prerequisite — allocate the part number, create the item with its Part Number Assignment and GitLab EC link, then return to create the BOM. See Creating a New Item.
--- ## Related Pages - Part Number Allocation — getting a controlled part number - Creating a New Item — creating the producible item with its EC link - BOM Structure & Search — finding and reading BOMs, the balloon callout report - Product Bundles — kits, which use the same signoff gate - InductOne Build Pipeline — how BOMs are consumed in a build --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---

content:
Roles & Permissions
ERPNext Access Control · Who Can Do What
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** System Managers adding or modifying users. Anyone who needs to understand what access a given role provides. Operations staff who need to understand why they can or cannot perform a specific action. --- ## Role Architecture The role model is intentionally modular. Roles should be assigned for the job a person actually performs; access should not be borrowed from broad legacy profiles. The current role categories are: | Category | Role / profile | Purpose | |---|---|---| | System ownership | System Manager; InductOne Process Architect | Full system administration and InductOne process architecture. | | InductOne process execution | InductOne Manager | Create builds, run release workflow, allocate serials, upload/review/accept completions. | | Engineering approval | Engineering User | Engineering Signoff and part-number allocation. | | Operations execution | Operations Manager | Normal ERPNext operations workflow: items/BOMs/orders/delivery/stock work, without InductOne authority by itself. | | Inventory execution | Inventory Operator | Receiving, stock movements, inventory transactions. | | Gripper manufacturing | Gripper Manufacturer | Serialized gripper work/refurbishment workflows. | | Read-only business visibility | Operations Viewer; Global Viewer | Operations-scoped read-only visibility, or universal read/report/export visibility without write authority. | | Procurement maintenance | Procurement User | Vendor/price/item purchasing metadata maintenance. | | External supplier | InductOne External Builder | Scoped builder portal access only. | InductOne Manager and InductOne Process Architect are active roles. Do not treat them as retired aliases. --- ## User → Role Assignments Representative production assignments follow this pattern: | User group | Target roles | |---|---| | System administrator | System Manager |`n| System owner / process architect | InductOne Process Architect, Engineering User, Operations Manager | | InductOne process operators | InductOne Manager; add Engineering User or Operations Manager only when that person also performs those duties | | Engineering approvers | Engineering User | | Operations managers | Operations Manager | | Inventory operators | Inventory Operator | | Gripper manufacturing users | Gripper Manufacturer, normally with Operations Manager when they also run broader operations work | | Finance / audit / executive read-only | Global Viewer | | Procurement | Procurement User | | Internal read-only users | Operations Viewer | | External builders / suppliers | InductOne External Builder plus supplier User Permission scoping | Role profiles are not the source of truth for this hardened model. Clear broad profiles before assigning these roles directly. --- ## Role Permissions — What Each Role Can Do ### System Manager Full ERPNext administration. This role can administer users, roles, fixtures, customizations, and all InductOne records. Use sparingly. ### InductOne Process Architect Owns the InductOne process design. Can approve Engineering Signoffs, allocate part numbers, manage process-level InductOne configuration, and access Fixture Export Control where permitted. This is an active high-trust role, not a deprecated alias. ### InductOne Manager Runs the InductOne workflow: builds, configuration orders, configured snapshots, BOM export packages, builder release, serial allocation, build completions, review/rejection, and acceptance. This role does not by itself grant broad ERPNext item/BOM/sales-order editing. ### Engineering User Reviews and approves Engineering Signoffs and allocates part numbers. Engineering User can approve/reject signoffs for BOM, Item, Product Bundle, and InductOne Configuration Option records. It does not grant broad operations write authority by itself. ### Operations Viewer Read-only internal operations visibility: items, BOMs, sales/delivery/stock context, InductOne records, reports, and audit trails. No create/edit/submit authority. ### Global Viewer Universal read/report/export visibility for finance, audit, and executive review, including financial reports, stock reports, operational records, and system configuration records that are safe for non-admin read access. No create/edit/submit authority. Framework metadata such as DocType and Custom DocPerm remains System-Manager-only by Frappe design. ### Operations Manager Runs normal ERPNext operations: item/BOM/sales-order/delivery/stock workflows and production-facing operational updates. It does not grant InductOne release/acceptance authority unless paired with InductOne Manager. ### Inventory Operator Runs inventory transactions such as receiving and stock movements, including the serial/batch dependency permissions required for those transactions. ### Gripper Manufacturer Runs serialized gripper manufacturing/refurbishment workflows and related serial/batch transaction dependencies. ### Procurement User Maintains purchasing/vendor-facing metadata such as item purchase details and item prices. Can create and view Purchase Orders, but does not own Purchase Order submission or accounting authority. ### InductOne External Builder External suppliers get Builder Portal access only. Custom DocPerms grant this role exactly the scoped InductOne Configuration Order and InductOne Build Completion access needed for released work and completion upload. Supplier User Permissions restrict each builder to its own supplier records. External builders do **not** receive raw Item, BOM, Sales Order, Field Change, or general ERPNext workspace access. They should see only released/scoped handoff records and the completion path required to return evidence. --- ## Special Accounts **quickbooks.integration** — The QuickBooks integration service account. Should carry only `Accounts User` and whatever ERPNext permissions the QB sync requires. Should not carry any InductOne, Operations, or legacy gatekeeping roles. Audit this account periodically. **Legacy test accounts** — Any test accounts that are no longer needed should be disabled entirely in ERPNext. Do not leave test accounts active with incorrect role assignments. --- ## Adding a New User 1. Identify the work the user is actually expected to perform. 2. Clear broad role profiles unless there is an explicit System Manager decision. 3. Assign the smallest set of modular roles that matches the work. 4. For external suppliers, assign InductOne External Builder and the required Supplier User Permission only. 5. Smoke-test the user with the relevant workflow before treating access as complete. --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---

content:
InductOne CSA Owner Handbook
Configuration · Release · Builder Completion · As-Built · Instance · Audit
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** InductOne Managers, Engineering Users, Process Architects, System Managers, and backup owners who need to operate or audit the complete InductOne CSA workflow without relying on tribal memory. ## Operating principle InductOne is controlled by explicit workflow gates. Users should not manually bypass status, signoff, serial allocation, builder release state, builder completion review, or as-built acceptance. If a record cannot proceed, fix the upstream missing artifact or approval rather than editing around the gate. ## Visual operating maps
End-to-end CSA workflow
CSA quality system map
Configuration option status gate
Builder package composition
As-built and instance lineage
## The controlled chain 1. **Engineering intent is made controllable** through Items, BOMs, Product Bundles, Configuration Options, and Engineering Signoffs. 2. **A build becomes a machine configuration** when released options are selected on an InductOne Build and resolved into a Configured BOM Snapshot. 3. **The release package is created** as a Configuration Order plus BOM Export Package, document index, serial allocation, balloon callout report, and builder completion workbook. 4. **The builder works from the released package** and returns completion evidence through the Build Completion intake. 5. **Operations accepts or rejects completion**; acceptance creates the As-Built Record and InductOne Instance. 6. **The live machine record is maintained** through deviations, FCOs, audits, and future as-maintained updates. ## Owner navigation - Start with [InductOne CSA Quality System](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-csa-quality-system) for the controlled-doc-to-ERPNext map. - Use [InductOne CSA Controlled Records Index](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-csa-controlled-records-index) to locate source documents and future release links. - Use [InductOne Build Pipeline](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-build-pipeline) for daily execution. - Use [Configuration Options](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/configuration-options), [Engineering Signoff](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/engineering-signoff), and [InductOne Snapshot Diff Tool](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-snapshot-diff-tool) for option and baseline governance. - Use [Serialization Rules and Part Number Allocation](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/serialization-rules), [BOM Export Package](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/bom-export-package), and [As-Built Records and Instances](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/as-built-records-and-instances) for serial custody and handoff evidence. - Use [Deviation Requests](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/deviation-requests) when a one-time departure or field change must be recorded without silently changing the baseline. ## Controlled source alignment The Wiki summarizes the CSA corpus but does not replace it. The controlling document families are: `OPS-CSA-PLN-001`, `OPS-SER-POL-001`, `OPS-CFG-CON-01`, `OPS-CFG-DEV-01`, `OPS-CSA-AUD-01`, `OPS-LBL-G01`, `WI-OPS-BLD-001`, the Option Matrix, CI Index, Serialized Prefix Register, FCO templates/registers, and Builder Completion Serial Workbook. ## Policy of record -- field-side FCO intake Field-side POR Field Change / Deviation intake is distinct from formal pre-release build/configuration deviation control, which is handled procedurally rather than by a separate ERPNext workflow (there is no in-ERPNext Deviation Request DocType). The field-side intake flow is: POR Field Change / Deviation intake form (JotForm) → Operations Engineering triage → disposition path. A one-time post-shipment field change becomes an FCO on `OPS-FCO-T01` or `OPS-FCO-T02`; a pre-delivery departure follows the deviation procedure in `OPS-CFG-DEV-01`; a repeated cross-site condition escalates to ECR through the Engineering Change process. The accepted intake is registered in `SUP-FCO-R01`, dispositioned, implemented, completed including any required ERPNext as-maintained update, and then closed in the register. `SUP-FCO-R01_FCORegister-v2-0.xlsx` is the controlled schema template; the operating register instance location is to be designated. ## Policy of record -- option visibility, validation snapshots, and acceptance gates **Option visibility is policy, not convenience.** Catalog and print views show Released options by default. Draft and Deprecated options remain reachable only through explicit filters for engineering and ownership work. Draft options are ideation records; they are not build-usable. Engineering Signoff is the release gate that promotes an approved option into the Released catalog. **Validation snapshots are generated on scratch builds only; a real Build's snapshot history is a clean audit trail.** Validation, smoke testing, and report proofing must clone or create a dedicated scratch/test Build before creating Configured BOM Snapshots. Real customer Build snapshot history is reserved for production release evidence. **Build Completion acceptance is a two-step gate with no dual-control requirement.** Reviewed = data check: the uploaded workbook parses, serial rows are complete, and serial rows are well-formed. Accepted = evidence check: Operations verifies serial/label evidence against the returned workbook and confirms the configuration match. Acceptance creates the locked As-Built Record and InductOne Instance. The same authorized person may review and accept when appropriate; a reviewer-not-equal-acceptor constraint is not part of this system. ## What “done correctly” looks like - Every released machine has an as-designed snapshot and configuration order. - Every builder handoff has a serial allocation, document index, package manifest, and completion workbook path. - Every accepted completion creates an immutable as-built record and a deployed instance. - Every one-time departure is tied to a deviation/concession/waiver or FCO, not hidden in free-text edits. - Every audit discrepancy has a disposition path: data correction, FCO/as-maintained update, or Engineering Change action. ## FCO / as-installed owner policy Policy of record: ERPNext is the operating system of record for InductOne as-installed / as-maintained status. The `InductOne Instance` record is the machine register. `POR Physical Location` is the canonical physical-location hierarchy at `Site → Lane → Cell`. Field Change intake is ERPNext-native after import. The intake source may be JotForm, but the controlled operating ledger is `InductOne Field Change Request`. The generated `SUP-FCO-R01` export is a report/export of ERPNext data, not the source of truth. Assignments are tracked-but-changeable. Historical/backfilled FCO-to-Instance assignments may be low-confidence. If Operations reassigns a Request to a different Instance, the user must enter an assignment-change reason; ERPNext Version history preserves the change. Field Change records are per-machine as-maintained events. Acceptance locks the Field Change, updates the linked Instance rollup, and preserves the original As-Built Record unchanged. SATX internal/reference segmentation is: - `Display / Testing`: CEC and Golden Unit internal/reference records. - `Lab`: Amazon POC and other outbound/transient test units that may eventually leave POR.

content:
InductOne CSA Quality System
Controlled Documents · ERPNext System of Record · Audit Trail
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** InductOne Process Architects, System Managers, backup process owners, Engineering approvers, and auditors who need to understand how the controlled CSA documentation maps to the live ERPNext implementation. > ⚠️ **Release-link note:** The controlled CSA documents are currently sourced from the local Quality-System corpus. Final SharePoint links should be inserted here after the documents are released through the Engineering Change process. Until then, this Wiki page is an operator guide and index — not a substitute for formal document release. ## CSA operating principle The InductOne CSA system has two layers: 1. **Controlled source layer** — the CSA plan, serialization policy, procedures, registers, templates, and FCO records define what the system is required to do. 2. **ERPNext execution layer** — `inductone_tools` turns those requirements into governed records, workflow gates, generated packages, serial allocation, builder completion intake, as-built records, instances, and audit evidence.
## What the system accounts for | CSA state | ERPNext record family | What it proves | |---|---|---| | As-designed | Configured BOM Snapshot, Build Option Selection, Configuration Order | The approved option set and resolved BOM for a specific machine before release. | | Released to builder | Configuration Order, BOM Export Package, document index, serial allocation | The exact package issued to the supplier, including the machine serial and controlled handoff artifacts. | | As-built | InductOne Build Completion, As-Built Record, component serial rows | The supplier-returned evidence and Operations acceptance of what was actually built. | | As-maintained | InductOne Instance, FCO/deviation/audit records | The living post-delivery record after field changes, corrections, or audit findings. | ## Operator pages connected to this system - [InductOne CSA Owner Handbook](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-csa-owner-handbook) — the owner/operator map for the end-to-end process. - [Configuration Options](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/configuration-options) — option lifecycle, released-only selection, and BOM mapping semantics. - [InductOne Build Pipeline](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-build-pipeline) — build creation through builder handoff and completion. - [Serialization Rules and Part Number Allocation](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/serialization-rules) — serial allocation, prefixes, and labeling custody. - [Deviation Requests](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/deviation-requests) — one-time departures, concessions, waivers, and FCO escalation. - [As-Built Records and Instances](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/as-built-records-and-instances) — acceptance, immutable as-built records, and deployed instance custody. - [InductOne Snapshot Diff Tool](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-snapshot-diff-tool) — evidence for what changed between configured baselines. - [BOM Export Package](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/bom-export-package) — builder package composition and document index. > **Read-me-first entry point:** `OPS-CSA-OVW-01_SystemOverviewAndOrientation-v1-0.docx` is the newcomer orientation for the CSA system. Read it before the procedure set; the remaining controlled documents explain the specific operating controls. ## Controlled document families | Family | Representative source documents | ERPNext implementation touchpoint | |---|---|---| | CSA plan | `OPS-CSA-PLN-001` | Defines as-designed/as-built/as-maintained states and the ERPNext system of record. | | Serialization policy | `OPS-SER-POL-001`, `OPS-SER-R01` | Serial tranche allocation, `IND-####` machine serials, component serial capture. | | Configuration model | `OPS-CFG-CON-01`, `OPS-CFG-M01`, `OPS-CFG-R01` | Option catalog, structural effects, configured snapshots, configuration orders. | | Deviation/FCO control | `OPS-CFG-DEV-01`, `OPS-FCO-T01/T02`, FCO registers | One-time departures and post-ship as-maintained configuration changes. | | Audit | `OPS-CSA-AUD-01` | Quarterly physical-vs-ERPNext record verification and discrepancy disposition. | | Builder integration | `WI-OPS-BLD-001`, `OPS-BLD-F01` | Builder package handoff, completion workbook, serial evidence, acceptance. | | Labeling | `OPS-LBL-G01` | Label material, format, placement, and application rules for serialized items. | ## Fixture ownership This page is repo-owned through `inductone_tools/fixtures/wiki_page.json` and the exact-name `Wiki Page` fixture filter in `hooks.py`. Do not edit this page directly in production without exporting the result back into the app fixture.

Policy of record: Catalog and print views show Released options by default. Draft and Deprecated options are reachable only through explicit filters for engineering and owner review; Draft options are not usable in released build workflows and are promoted to Released only through the Engineering Signoff gate.
Configuration Options
Catalog Governance · Option Lifecycle · BOM Mapping Actions
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations Engineers who need to understand what options are, how they become available in the build catalog, and how they translate into BOM changes. Engineers and Operations who govern the option catalog. --- ## What a Configuration Option Is An `InductOne Configuration Option` is a selectable feature that a machine can have. Each option represents a discrete hardware or configuration choice — a gripper variant, a conveyor orientation modifier, an interface kit, a cable run, or any other itemized difference between one build and another. Options are the bridge between the customer's order (what they asked for) and the BOM (what actually gets built). When an operator selects options on a build, the snapshot generator uses each selected option's **mapping actions** to modify the baseline 150% BOM into the exact configured BOM for that machine. Without options, every build would use the same flat BOM. With options, each build can be precisely and traceably different from every other. --- ## Option Catalog Location **Navigate to:** InductOne Configuration Option list --- ## Option Identity Fields Each option record carries the following identity fields: | Field | Purpose | |---|---| | `option_code` | Short code used in audit trails, delta lines, and reports. Used as the `source_option_code` throughout the snapshot and CO | | `option_name` | Human-readable name shown in the build's selections table and CO | | `option_category` | Grouping for display purposes (e.g. "Gripper", "Interface", "Conveyor") | | `option_group` | Mutual exclusion group. Only one option per group can be selected on a build | | `option_group_required` | If checked, exactly one option in this group must be selected before snapshot generation proceeds | | `is_default_selection` | If checked, this option is auto-selected when Load Catalog Options runs, for its group | | `is_active` | Whether this option appears in the catalog at all | | `status` | Lifecycle governance status — controls whether this option loads into build selections | | `internal_notes` | Engineering context visible to Ops but not included in builder-facing documents | | `builder_description` | Description included in the Configuration Order for the builder | | `mapping_status` | Manual completeness flag — tracks whether the option's BOM mappings have been fully defined | --- ## Option Lifecycle > **Policy of record:** Catalog and print views show Released options by default. Draft and Deprecated options remain reachable only through explicit filters for engineering and ownership work. Draft options are ideation records and are not build-usable. Engineering Signoff is the only release gate that promotes an approved option into the Released catalog. Options follow the three statuses that exist in ERPNext: | Status | Meaning | Build-usable? | |---|---|---| | `Draft` | The option is being ideated or mapped. Mapping rows may still change. | No | | `Released` | The option passed Engineering Signoff, is locked for direct edit, and loads into build selections. | Yes | | `Deprecated` | The option is retired for new work but remains available for audit/history on records that already used it. | No | The release path is deliberately narrow: create or revise the option in `Draft`, complete its mapping rows, set `mapping_status = Complete`, request Engineering Signoff, and have an authorized approver approve the signoff. Approval performs the release transition and locks the option. Manual Draft → Released saves are blocked by the signoff gate. **Who can release by approving the signoff:** Engineering User, InductOne Process Architect, or System Manager. **How released options are revised:** use Supersede. Supersede creates a new Draft successor and marks the original option Deprecated so the historical record remains intact. Do not hand-retire a Released option by manually clearing active flags. Only `Released` options load into the build catalog when an operator clicks **Load Catalog Options**. Draft and Deprecated options are invisible to the build form unless someone intentionally filters for them in the option list. --- ## Option Groups and Mutual Exclusion Options are organized into **option groups**. The `option_group` field on each option record defines which group it belongs to. **Group exclusivity is enforced at the form level:** when an operator selects an option, any other option in the same group is automatically deselected. Only one option per group can be selected on a build at any time. **`option_group_required`:** When this checkbox is set on any option within a group, the entire group is considered required. At least one option in the group must be selected before snapshot generation, CO generation, or release can proceed. The validator `por_validate_option_group_requirements` enforces this and throws with a specific message listing the group name and its available options if the requirement is not met. **`is_default_selection`:** When set, this option is automatically selected when **Load Catalog Options** runs, for its group. Only one option per group should carry this flag. The auto-select pass only fires for required groups where no option is already selected. --- ## Mapping Actions — How Options Change the BOM Each option has a child table of **mapping actions** (`InductOne Configuration Option Mapping`). These define the exact BOM operations the snapshot generator applies when this option is selected. ### The Four Actions
Action What It Does Common Use
ADD Adds an item or BOM subtree to the configured BOM. Increments quantity if item already present Adding an optional kit, interface card, or accessory not in the baseline
REMOVE Removes an item or BOM branch from the configured BOM Removing a placeholder item from the 150% baseline that doesn't apply to this configuration
REPLACE Removes a target item/branch and adds a replacement item/branch in its place Swapping one gripper variant for another, or substituting a regional power supply
QTY_OVERRIDE Sets the quantity of an item to a specific value, overriding whatever the BOM says Adjusting cable lengths or consumable quantities for specific configurations
### Expand Modes Each mapping row also specifies an `expand_mode` that determines how the `target_item` is interpreted: | Expand Mode | Behavior | |---|---| | `AS_ITEM_ONLY` | Operates on the item as a single flat item. Does not expand any BOM. Default for most operations | | `EXPLODE_DEFAULT_BOM` | Explodes the target item's default active BOM to its leaves and operates on all leaf items | | `USE_TARGET_BOM` | Explodes a specific named BOM (the `target_bom` field) to its leaves | ### Structural Effect Mode The `structural_effect_mode` field defines how the mapping row affects the BOM hierarchy tree (as opposed to the flat item list). Most rows leave this as `AUTO`, which the system resolves based on the action and expand mode: | Action + Expand Mode | Auto-Resolved Effect | |---|---| | `REMOVE` + `AS_ITEM_ONLY` | `SUPPRESS_TARGET_NODE` | | `REMOVE` + `EXPLODE_DEFAULT_BOM` or `USE_TARGET_BOM` | `SUPPRESS_TARGET_BRANCH` | | `REPLACE` + `AS_ITEM_ONLY` | `REPLACE_TARGET_NODE` | | `REPLACE` + BOM expansion | `REPLACE_TARGET_BRANCH` | | `ADD` + `AS_ITEM_ONLY` | `INCREMENT_NODE_QTY` | | `ADD` + BOM expansion | `ADD_BRANCH` | | `QTY_OVERRIDE` | `OVERRIDE_NODE_QTY` | ### Row Order Mapping rows are applied in `row_order` sequence (ascending). The conventional spacing is 10 / 20 / 30 / 40 to leave room for insertions. Order matters when one mapping depends on the result of another — for example, a REMOVE followed by an ADD on the same item behaves differently depending on which fires first. --- ## What Happens When Load Catalog Options Runs When an operator clicks **Load Catalog Options** on an InductOne Build: 1. The system fetches active options with `status = Released`, ordered by `sort_order`. 2. Each option is added to the build's selections table. 3. Required groups are evaluated by `option_group_required`. 4. Default selections are applied for required groups where no selection has already been made. 5. Snapshot generation refuses to proceed if a required option group has no selected Released option. This means Draft options can be modeled and reviewed safely without becoming build-usable. The Engineering Signoff approval is the controlled transition into the released catalog. --- ## Adding or Modifying Options — Governance **To add a new option:** create the option in `Draft`, define the mapping rows, test the option's resolved BOM effect, and set `mapping_status = Complete` once the mappings are ready for review. Then request Engineering Signoff. Approval by Engineering User, InductOne Process Architect, or System Manager releases and locks the option. **To revise a Released option:** use Supersede. The system creates a Draft successor for the new definition and marks the old option Deprecated. Historical snapshots and configuration orders continue to point at the option version they used. **Do not directly edit Released options.** Released records are controlled build inputs. The safe edit path is supersession plus signoff. --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · Configuration Options Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment The configuration option model is the ERPNext implementation of `OPS-CFG-CON-01` and the Option Matrix (`OPS-CFG-M01`). Draft options are allowed for ideation, but they are not selectable for production builds. The governed path is Draft → Engineering Signoff → Released, with Deprecated retained for audit history. Operationally, this page should be read with [InductOne CSA Quality System](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-csa-quality-system) and [InductOne Snapshot Diff Tool](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-snapshot-diff-tool). The option catalog defines permitted choices; the snapshot proves the resolved as-designed baseline. ## CSA visual reference

content:
InductOne Build Pipeline
End-to-End Procedure · Sales Order → Completed Instance
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations Engineers running builds through the pipeline. If you are a PM looking for build status, see the Manufacturing Overview or the Operations Hub number cards. If you are Engineering reviewing a signoff, see Engineering Signoff. > ⚠️ **Before you start:** Every stage in this pipeline is gated by the previous one. Do not skip stages, work around validation errors by editing records manually, or try to advance a build by directly changing field values. If you are blocked by a gate, the gate is telling you something is wrong. Contact Operations. --- ## Prerequisites Before creating a build, confirm the following are in place: - A submitted Sales Order exists with the InductOne line item correctly specified - The top-level BOM for this machine variant exists, is active, is submitted (`docstatus = 1`), and has a current Engineering Signoff - All child BOMs in the tree that will be included in this configuration have current Engineering Signoffs - The builder supplier record exists in ERPNext - The relevant Configuration Options for this build are in `Released` status in the catalog --- ## Stage 1 — Create the Build **Navigate to:** Sales Order list → open the relevant SO On the Sales Order form, locate the InductOne line item row. Use the **Create InductOne Build** button (in the InductOne button group on the SO form) to spawn a new Build record linked to that line item. The system creates an `InductOne Build` record pre-populated with: - The Sales Order link and line item index - The customer project label (if set on the SO) - `build_status` = DRAFT **Set these fields before proceeding:** | Field | What to Enter | |---|---| | `top_item` | The InductOne top-level item code for this machine variant | | `top_bom` | The active, submitted BOM for the top item (filtered automatically to active submitted BOMs) | | `builder_supplier` | The supplier record for the external builder building this unit | | `orientation` | LH (left-hand) or RH (right-hand) — determines physical cell layout | > 💡 `top_bom` is constrained by a query filter to active, submitted BOMs only. If the BOM you expect does not appear, check that it is both `is_active = 1` and `docstatus = 1` in the BOM list. --- ## Stage 2 — Load Catalog Options **Button:** InductOne → **Load Catalog Options** This loads all active Configuration Options with status `Released` into the `selections` child table on the Build form. Each row represents one configurable feature the machine can have. After loading, the selections table shows every available option with `selected = 0` by default. Options that are marked `is_default_selection` and belong to a required option group are auto-selected. **What to do next:** Review each option group and make selections per the customer order: - Check `selected` on each option the customer has ordered - Within a group, only one option can be selected — checking a second automatically unchecks the first (group exclusivity enforced by the form) - Required option groups must have exactly one selection before you can proceed. The snapshot generator will throw if a required group has no selection > ⚠️ You can re-run Load Catalog Options if new options have been added to the catalog since you first loaded. It will not duplicate existing rows — it skips options already present in the selections table. --- ## Stage 3 — Generate Snapshot **Button:** InductOne → **Generate Snapshot** This is the most computationally significant step in the pipeline. The snapshot generator: 1. Resolves the top BOM (using `top_bom` from the build, or finding the active default if not set) 2. Explodes the full BOM tree to leaf items and rolls up quantities — this is the **baseline map** 3. For each selected option, fetches its mapping actions (ADD / REMOVE / REPLACE / QTY_OVERRIDE) and applies them to the baseline map in `row_order` sequence 4. Produces the **final map** — the exact flat item list for this configured machine 5. Records a **delta audit** of every change made by every option (what was added, removed, replaced, or overridden, and why) 6. Records **structural effects** — the BOM tree operations (suppress node, replace branch, add branch, etc.) that represent what the hierarchy will look like 7. Creates a `Configured BOM Snapshot` DocType record with all of the above frozen permanently 8. Materializes the full BOM hierarchy tree onto the snapshot (`populate_snapshot_hierarchy`) 9. Generates a BOM Hierarchy XLSX workbook attached to the snapshot (`generate_hierarchy_workbook`) 10. Updates the Build with `latest_snapshot` and increments `snapshot_rev` **The snapshot is immutable from the moment it is created.** Even if the underlying BOMs or Configuration Options change afterward, this snapshot record is a permanent record of what the configuration resolved to at this point in time. **You can generate multiple snapshots.** Each increments `snapshot_rev`. The Build tracks both `latest_snapshot` (most recently generated) and `selected_snapshot` (the one to use for the CO). These are typically the same unless you deliberately chose an earlier snapshot. > 💡 If you need to change option selections, you can do so and regenerate a new snapshot. The old snapshot is preserved. Use **Set Selected Snapshot = Latest Snapshot** to point the Build at the new one before generating the CO. > ⚠️ Option selections cannot be changed after the build reaches `RELEASED_TO_BUILDER`. The form locks at that point. --- ## Stage 4 — Generate Configuration Order **Button:** InductOne → **Generate Configuration Order** Creates an `InductOne Configuration Order` (CO) record from the selected snapshot. The CO is the formal order document that travels with this build through release and completion. The CO is populated with: - Links to the Build, Snapshot, Sales Order, and Builder Supplier - The list of selected options with codes, names, categories, and builder descriptions - The delta lines from the snapshot (material audit trail) - The `system_serial` if already allocated on the build (backfilled later if not yet allocated) - A Document Index pre-seeded with the BOM Export Package reference (if one exists) After CO creation, the system automatically calls `sync_hierarchy_workbook_to_configuration_order` to add the BOM hierarchy workbook from the snapshot into the CO's Document Index. The CO starts in status Draft and the form routes you directly to the new CO record. **CO Status Lifecycle:** | CO Status | Meaning | |---|---| | `Draft` | CO created, not yet released | | `Released` | Build released to builder. Record Builder Acknowledgement button appears | | `Awaiting Completion` | Builder acknowledged receipt. Upload Builder Completion button appears | | `Closed` | Completion accepted. CO permanently closed | > ⚠️ If you already have a CO and regenerate one (e.g. after re-snapshotting), a new CO is created with an incremented `config_order_rev`. The old CO is not deleted. The Build's `latest_config_order` pointer updates to the new one. --- ## Stage 5 — Generate BOM Export Package **Button:** InductOne → **Generate BOM Export Package** Opens or creates a `BOM Export Package` record for this build. If one already exists for this exact build / CO / snapshot combination, it opens the existing one. Otherwise it creates a new one pre-populated from the build. **On the BOM Export Package form**, run the generation process to produce the output ZIP. The package contains: - The flat configured BOM (Excel) - PDFs for each item in the BOM that has a schematic attached (watermarked with release metadata) - Work instructions - Balloon report - The OPS-BLD-F01 serial capture workbook — pre-populated with the system serial and empty component serial fields for the builder to fill in The package record links back to the Build and CO and is registered in the CO Document Index. > 💡 The **Generate BOM Export Package** button is an open-or-create operation — it will not create duplicates. If you click it multiple times, it opens the same package each time (matched on build + CO + snapshot). --- ## Stage 6 — Allocate Serial **Button:** Actions → **Allocate Serial** This button appears on the Build form when: - `builder_supplier` is set - `build_status` is `DRAFT` or `READY` - `system_serial` is not yet set Clicking it first **previews** the serial that would be assigned, then asks for confirmation before committing. The confirmation dialog is explicit: *"Serial allocation is one-way — it cannot be unset through the UI."* Once allocated: - `system_serial` is set on the Build - A blue indicator badge shows `Serial: [serial]` on the build form dashboard - The serial is embedded into the OPS-BLD-F01 workbook when the BOM Export Package is generated, so the builder knows what to stencil on the unit > ⚠️ **Serial allocation must happen before Release to Builder.** The acceptance function at the end of the pipeline requires `system_serial` to be present and will throw if it is missing. Do not skip this step. --- ## Stage 7 — Prepare Builder Handoff **Button:** InductOne → **Prepare Builder Handoff** This is a **read-only readiness check**. It does not generate any artifacts, mutate any records, or advance any status. It calls `check_builder_release_readiness` on the server and displays the result as a popup. **What it validates:** - Builder supplier is set - All required option groups have a selection - A snapshot exists and is selected - A Configuration Order exists and was generated from the selected snapshot (snapshot mismatch will fail) - A BOM Export Package exists and has been generated (output ZIP exists) - Engineering Signoff is current for the controlled release inputs: top BOM, top Item, Product Bundle when present, and selected Configuration Options The popup shows either: - ✓ Ready to release — all checks passed, proceed to Release to Builder - ✗ Not ready to release — one or more items listed that must be resolved first Warnings (non-blocking) may also appear alongside a passing result. > 💡 Run Prepare Builder Handoff before Release to Builder every time. It is the fastest way to confirm nothing was missed and gives you specific blocker text to act on if something is wrong. --- ## Stage 8 — Release to Builder **Button:** InductOne → **Release To Builder** This is the point of no return for a build's configuration. The release function: 1. Re-runs `por_validate_release_basis` client-side (fast pre-flight: supplier, option groups, snapshot, CO, snapshot/CO alignment) 2. Verifies the BOM Export Package has a generated output ZIP 3. Calls `release_to_builder_now` server-side, which performs its own deep readiness check 4. Stamps `released_at`, `released_by`, and `build_status = RELEASED_TO_BUILDER` on the Build 5. Transitions the CO to status `Released` 6. Locks the build form — `builder_supplier`, `top_item`, `top_bom`, `orientation`, `selections`, and `selected_snapshot` all become read-only After release, the **Record Builder Acknowledgement** button appears (driven by CO status = `Released`). > ⚠️ There is no "undo release" function. If a released build needs to be reworked, contact Operations to discuss the SUPERSEDED flow. --- ## Stage 9 — Record Builder Acknowledgement **Button:** InductOne → **Record Builder Acknowledgement** Appears after release when `co_status = Released`. Opens a dialog to log that the builder has received and accepted the package: - **Acknowledgement File** (optional) — attach a signed return of the release PDF, an email PDF, or other written evidence - **Note** (optional) — context such as "acknowledged via email from [contact name]" On confirmation: - CO status transitions to `Awaiting Completion` - Timestamp and user are stamped on the CO - If a file was attached, it is added to the CO Document Index - The **Upload Builder Completion** button now appears > 💡 A file is not required. If the builder acknowledges verbally or via email and you have no PDF to attach, record the acknowledgement with a note describing the contact method. The system does not block on a missing file. --- ## Stage 10 — Upload Builder Completion **Button:** InductOne → **Upload Builder Completion** Appears after acknowledgement when `co_status = Awaiting Completion`. Opens a dialog to submit the builder's returned serial workbook: - **Builder Serial Workbook** (required) — the returned OPS-BLD-F01 or equivalent serial capture document - **Submitted By** — builder contact name - **Builder Reference** — builder's internal job or traveler number (optional) - **Completion Notes** (optional) On submission, the system: 1. Creates an `InductOne Build Completion` record 2. Parses the workbook and populates component serial rows 3. Reports how many of the expected component serial rows were populated (`filled/total`) 4. Reports any serial warnings (missing, duplicate, or unexpected entries) 5. Routes you to the new Build Completion form If warnings are present, review them before proceeding. They indicate component serials that could not be parsed from the workbook. --- ## Stage 11 — Review Completion **On the InductOne Build Completion form** Review the parsed serial data against the returned workbook. Verify: - System serial matches the allocated serial on the Build - Component serial rows are populated and plausible - No unexpected warnings remain unresolved When satisfied, set the Build Completion status to `Reviewed`. This unlocks the Accept Completion step. > ⚠️ The Accept step requires `status = Reviewed` on the Build Completion. It will throw if the completion has not been reviewed. --- ## Stage 12 — Accept Completion **On the InductOne Build Completion form** (once status = `Reviewed`) Acceptance is a single atomic server-side operation that cannot be partially completed: 1. Creates the `InductOne As-Built Record` — permanently locked on creation. Contains all component serial data from the completion 2. Sets Build Completion status to `Accepted` 3. Sets `completion_status = Accepted` and links `as_built_record` on the Build 4. Sets `build_status = COMPLETED` on the Build 5. Transitions CO to `Closed` 6. Creates the `InductOne Instance` — the live deployed-unit record used by Support for the life of the machine If any precondition fails (no serials on the completion, Build already has an As-Built Record, `system_serial` missing on the Build, CO missing), the entire operation throws and nothing is committed. > ✅ After acceptance, the build is complete. The InductOne Instance is live and available to Support. The As-Built Record is the permanent, immutable record of what was built and is the authoritative CSA reference for this unit. --- ## Troubleshooting Common Blocks
Prepare Handoff shows "Engineering Signoff" failures One or more BOMs in the configured tree do not have a current Engineering Signoff. The readiness check walks the entire BOM tree recursively — not just the top BOM. **Resolution:** Contact Engineering (Signoff role) with the specific BOM names listed in the failure output. They must approve the pending signoffs before the build can be released. Do not modify controlled engineering records to work around the gate; route the blocker through the signoff process.
"Snapshot mismatch" error on Prepare or Release The Configuration Order was generated from a different snapshot than the one currently selected on the Build. This happens when you generate a new snapshot after already having a CO. **Resolution:** Either regenerate the CO from the current selected snapshot (Generate Configuration Order again), or use **Set Selected Snapshot = Latest Snapshot** and then regenerate.
BOM Export Package button opens a blank package with no output ZIP The package was created but generation has not been run yet, or generation failed. **Resolution:** Open the BOM Export Package form and run the generation process from there. Do not release to builder until `output_zip` is populated on the package.
Accept Completion throws "system_serial missing" The Build does not have a system serial allocated. This must be done before release. **Resolution:** If the build is still in DRAFT/READY, use **Allocate Serial** from the Actions menu. If the build has already been released, this is a process error — contact Operations to determine the correct path forward.
Build is stuck in RELEASED_TO_BUILDER with no completion workbook The builder has not yet returned the OPS-BLD-F01. This is a normal operational state — the build waits here until the builder completes the unit. **Resolution:** Follow up with the builder directly. When the workbook is received, use Upload Builder Completion.
--- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · InductOne Build Pipeline Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment The build pipeline is the live execution of `OPS-CFG-CON-01`, `WI-OPS-BLD-001`, and the CSA plan. The key custody handoff is release to builder: serial allocation, Configuration Order, BOM Export Package, document index, balloon callout report, and builder completion workbook are generated or issued as the released build package. Use [InductOne CSA Owner Handbook](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-csa-owner-handbook) for the owner-level map and [BOM Export Package](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/bom-export-package) for the package contents. ## CSA visual reference

Serialization Rules & Part Number Allocation
System Serial · Component Lineage · Tranche Governance
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations Engineers allocating serials and managing builder tranches. Anyone who needs to understand how InductOne units are identified, how component serials are tracked, and how the serial numbering system is governed. --- ## Two Levels of Serialization InductOne serialization operates at two distinct levels: **Level 1 — System Serial (`IND-XXXX`):** The top-level identifier for the entire machine. Stenciled physically on the unit at the builder. This is the serial Support, customers, and field teams use to identify a machine. One per machine, permanently assigned. **Level 2 — Component Serials:** Individual serialized components inside the machine — cameras, PLCs, robot arms, grippers, IPCs, and other tracked assemblies. Captured by the builder in the OPS-BLD-F01 workbook during assembly and returned to Plus One on build completion. These two levels serve different purposes. The system serial identifies the machine as a whole. Component serials provide traceability into the machine — what specific hardware is inside, from what receipt, from what supplier. --- ## System Serial — Format and Source **Format:** `IND-` followed by a four-digit integer. Examples: `IND-2001`, `IND-2042`, `IND-3000`. **Source:** Issued from the active Builder Tranche for the builder assigned to the build. The integer portion comes from the tranche's `next_serial` counter, which increments atomically on each allocation. **Where it lives in ERPNext:** - `system_serial` field on `InductOne Build` — set at allocation time - Carried onto `InductOne Configuration Order` when the CO is generated (or backfilled when allocated after CO generation) - Pre-populated in the OPS-BLD-F01 workbook so the builder knows what to stencil - Stamped on `InductOne As-Built Record` at acceptance - Used as the document name for `InductOne Instance` (e.g. the Instance record is named `IND-2042`) --- ## Builder Tranches The tranche system governs which serial ranges belong to which builder. Each `InductOne Builder Tranche` record defines a non-overlapping integer range assigned to one builder. **DocType:** `InductOne Builder Tranche`
Field Description
builder_supplier The Supplier record for the builder this tranche belongs to
status Active or Retired. Only Active tranches can issue serials
tranche_label Human-readable label (e.g. "2000 series", "Builder Bravo — initial run")
tranche_start / tranche_end Inclusive integer bounds. e.g. 2000–2999 for a 1,000-unit tranche
next_serial The next integer to issue. Increments atomically on each allocation. When next_serial = tranche_end + 1, the tranche is exhausted
serial_prefix Always IND. Produces IND-XXXX formatted serials
### Tranche Validation Rules The system enforces these rules on every tranche save: - `tranche_end` must be ≥ `tranche_start` - `next_serial` must be within `[tranche_start, tranche_end + 1]` - **No range overlap with any other tranche, active or retired.** The system queries all existing tranches and rejects any new or modified tranche whose range intersects an existing one — even a Retired one. This is absolute: the same integer can never appear in two tranches, ever. The no-overlap rule is the critical one. It means that even if a builder is retired and their tranche is marked Retired, that integer range is permanently claimed. A future builder cannot be assigned overlapping numbers. ### Managing Tranches **To add a new builder:** Create a new `InductOne Builder Tranche` record with a fresh non-overlapping range. Set `status = Active`. Assign the correct `builder_supplier`. **To retire a builder:** Set the tranche `status = Retired`. The tranche cannot issue new serials. The range remains reserved permanently. **To exhaust a tranche and start a new one:** When `next_serial` reaches `tranche_end + 1`, the tranche is exhausted. Retire it and create a new Active tranche with a new range for the same builder (if they continue) or a new builder. **One Active tranche per builder at a time.** There is no hard system constraint enforcing this, but it is the operational rule. Having two Active tranches for the same builder creates ambiguity about which range to allocate from. --- ## Allocating a System Serial **When:** Before Release to Builder. The serial must be allocated while the build is in `DRAFT` or `READY` status. It cannot be allocated after release. **How:** On the Build form, the **Allocate Serial** button appears in the Actions menu when: - `builder_supplier` is set on the build - `build_status` is `DRAFT` or `READY` - `system_serial` is not yet set Clicking the button: 1. **Previews** the serial that would be assigned (calls `preview_serial_for_build` — read-only, does not commit) 2. Shows a confirmation dialog: *"Allocate serial IND-XXXX to this build? This will be stenciled on the physical unit by the builder. Serial allocation is one-way — it cannot be unset through the UI."* 3. On confirmation, calls `allocate_serial_for_build` which atomically: - Stamps `system_serial = IND-XXXX` on the Build - Increments `next_serial` on the active tranche 4. A blue `Serial: IND-XXXX` badge appears on the Build form dashboard Once allocated, the serial is visible throughout the pipeline. When the CO is generated (or if it already exists), `system_serial` is carried onto the CO record. When the BOM Export Package is generated, the OPS-BLD-F01 workbook is pre-populated with the serial. > ⚠️ **Serial allocation is one-way.** There is no UI to unset a serial. If allocated in error, both the Build field and the tranche's `next_serial` must be corrected via bench console — two steps together. Doing only one step leaves the data inconsistent and corrupts the audit trail. --- ## Component Serials — The OPS-BLD-F01 Workbook Component serials are captured through the builder completion workbook where the controlled serialization register requires them. The workbook is returned by the builder and parsed into Build Completion serial rows. Register convention: 5G / ConnectOne is represented as a configuration option and is not serialized as an InductOne component. Component serialization remains focused on the required controlled component families such as IPC (`POR####`), HMI (`HMI-####`), OD sensors (`OD-####`), grippers (`ICC-U` / `ICC-L`), robot controller/vendor identifiers, and RealSense vendor serials, as applicable to the build. **What happens when the workbook is uploaded:** When Operations clicks **Upload Builder Completion** and attaches the returned workbook, the system parses the component serial rows and populates the Build Completion's serial child table automatically where it can match the fields. Any rows it cannot parse are flagged as warnings. --- ## Part Number Conventions Part numbers (item codes) in ERPNext follow Plus One's established naming conventions. Key rules relevant to the InductOne pipeline: **Gripper blank convention:** The ICC gripper blank (the unassembled gripper body) uses a specific serial/labeling convention where the blank's label is reused for the parent gripper assembly. This is a known, documented workaround — the convention is captured in the As-Built serial rows with appropriate notes rather than being treated as a data error. **LH/RH (Left-hand / Right-hand) designation:** Many InductOne items come in mirrored left-hand and right-hand variants. The LH/RH suffix in item codes is meaningful and must be preserved exactly. The BOM revision identity matching system explicitly excludes `LH`/`RH` suffixes from revision pattern matching to avoid false matches between handed variants. **Revision suffixes:** Item codes may carry single-letter revision suffixes (e.g. `ITEM-001A`, `ITEM-001B`). The system's revision identity matching handles trailing single-letter revisions correctly, distinguishing them from the item base code while correctly grouping them as the same item family for comparison purposes. --- ## Audit and Traceability The serial system is designed to provide complete forward and backward traceability: **Forward (build → components):** From the Instance or As-Built Record, you can see every component serial that was inside the machine when it left the builder. **Backward (component → build):** Given a component serial, search the `InductOne As-Built Record` serial rows to find which machine it ended up in and which build produced it. **Tranche audit:** The Builder Tranche records show exactly which builder issued each serial range. Given any `IND-XXXX` serial, the tranche that issued it can be identified by finding the tranche whose `[tranche_start, tranche_end]` contains the integer. --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · Serialization Rules & Part Number Allocation Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment Serialization is governed by `OPS-SER-POL-001`, the Serialized Prefix Register (`OPS-SER-R01`), the Labeling Style Guide (`OPS-LBL-G01`), and the builder work instruction (`WI-OPS-BLD-001`). ERPNext is the system of record for allocated serials, machine identity, component serial capture, and the as-built chain. When the release process allocates an `IND-####` serial, that serial becomes the anchor for the builder package, completion workbook, as-built record, instance, and future FCO/audit trail. ## CSA visual reference
