Deviation Requests, Concessions, Waivers, and FCOs
One-Time Departures · Field Changes · As-Maintained Records
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations and Engineering users who need to record a one-time departure from the approved configuration, or determine whether a change belongs in the Engineering Change process or Field Change Order path. ## Why this exists A deviation is not a quiet edit to the BOM, snapshot, or as-built record. It is an auditable authorization to depart from the approved configuration for a defined scope. The source procedure is `OPS-CFG-DEV-01`, subordinate to the CSA plan. ## Field-side intake flow -- keep distinct from ERPNext deviation records The POR Field Change / Deviation intake form (JotForm) is the field-side intake path for post-shipment or field-discovered configuration issues. It does not replace formal pre-release build/configuration deviation control, which is governed procedurally (there is no separate in-ERPNext Deviation Request DocType). Field-side intake flow: 1. POR Field Change / Deviation intake form (JotForm) is submitted. 2. Operations Engineering triages the intake. 3. If it is a one-time post-shipment change, process it as an FCO using `OPS-FCO-T01` or `OPS-FCO-T02`. 4. If it is a pre-delivery departure from the approved configuration, process it as a deviation under `OPS-CFG-DEV-01`. 5. If the condition repeats across sites or should change the baseline, escalate it to ECR through the Engineering Change process. 6. Register accepted FCO/deviation intake in `SUP-FCO-R01`. 7. Record disposition, implement the approved action, complete the record including any required ERPNext as-maintained update, and close the item in the register. `SUP-FCO-R01_FCORegister-v2-0.xlsx` is the controlled schema template. The operating register is a separate record instance; operating instance location: to be designated. ## Choose the right path | Situation | Use | What it means | |---|---|---| | Departure needed before manufacture | Deviation | Prior authorization to build or release outside the current requirement for a defined unit/scope. | | Departure already exists and may be accepted | Concession | Authorization to accept product that already differs from requirement. | | Release outside baseline for limited scope | Waiver | Permission to release despite a requirement miss, with explicit scope/expiry. | | Change should become permanent for future units | ECR/ECO/ECN | Baseline-changing Engineering Change. Do not treat as a one-off. | | Post-ship hardware/configuration change | FCO | Updates the as-maintained record and links back to the affected instance. | ## ERPNext ledger model `InductOne Field Change Request` is the ERPNext ledger for field-side FCO intake. `SUP-FCO-R01` is generated from ERPNext as the controlled register export. The importer routes Field Change / ECR outcomes; procedural deviation handling remains distinct and should not be described as a separate ERPNext workflow. --- ## Minimum record content Every departure record should identify the affected `IND-####` / configuration item, describe the departure, state the reason, capture risk and disposition, name the disposition authority, record date/scope/expiry, and link to the relevant ERPNext CSA record. ## Non-negotiables - Deviations never override serialization obligations. - Do not use a deviation to sneak a permanent design change into one build. - If the same departure repeats, raise Engineering Change or revise the option/baseline model. - If the change affects the physical machine after shipment, create an FCO/as-maintained update path. ## Related pages - [InductOne CSA Quality System](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/inductone-csa-quality-system) - [As-Built Records and Instances](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/as-built-records-and-instances) - [Configuration Options](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/configuration-options) - [Engineering Signoff](/app/wiki/plus-one-ops-manual/engineering-signoff) ## Field-side FCO intake and triage The field-side FCO intake path is distinct from build/configuration deviation control. There is no separate in-ERPNext Deviation Request DocType in this app; deviation disposition is governed procedurally and recorded through the appropriate FCO/ECR/deviation record path. Field-side intake follows this controlled flow: `POR Field Change / Deviation intake form (JotForm) → Operations Engineering triage → ERPNext Field Change Request → disposition → implementation → as-maintained update → closure` Triage outcome decides the record path: | Triage outcome | Use when | ERPNext / external record | |---|---|---| | Field Change | A post-shipment machine needs a one-time field change or retrofit | `InductOne Field Change Request`, plus per-Instance `InductOne Field Change` records when implemented | | Deviation | A pre-delivery departure from the released configuration is needed | Deviation process per `OPS-CFG-DEV-01` | | ECR | The issue repeats across sites or changes the controlled baseline | Engineering Change process (`ECR → ECO → ECN`), referenced from ERPNext | ERPNext is now the operating ledger for FCO intake. JotForm submissions are imported into `InductOne Field Change Request` records using the JotForm importer. Each Request preserves the intake reference, requester, triage outcome, assignment confidence, assignment basis, and disposition. Low-confidence or backfill-guess assignments remain tracked-but-changeable: changing the assigned Instance requires an assignment-change reason and is captured in the Version log. When a Field Change is implemented for a specific machine, it creates a per-Instance `InductOne Field Change` record. Accepted Field Changes are locked and update the linked Instance as-maintained history.

As-Built Records & Instances
Build Closeout · Immutable As-Built · Deployed Unit Record
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations Engineers completing builds. Support Operations referencing deployed unit records. Anyone who needs to understand what an As-Built Record is, what an Instance is, and how both are created. --- ## Overview The final stage of the InductOne build pipeline creates two permanent records in a single atomic operation: - **`InductOne As-Built Record`** — the immutable, locked record of what was actually built. Contains every component serial captured in the builder's returned workbook. Created and locked at the moment of acceptance. Never modified. - **`InductOne Instance`** — the live deployed-unit record. Named `IND-XXXX` after the system serial stenciled on the physical machine. This is what Support references for the life of the unit. Both are created simultaneously when **Accept Completion** is triggered on a reviewed Build Completion record. If the operation fails for any reason, neither record is created — there is no partial state. --- ## The Build Completion Record Before acceptance can happen, an `InductOne Build Completion` record must exist and be in `Reviewed` status. **Build Completion is created** when Operations uploads the returned OPS-BLD-F01 workbook via **Upload Builder Completion** on the Build form. The system parses component serials from the workbook and populates the serial rows automatically where it can match them. Warnings are surfaced for rows that could not be parsed. **Build Completion status lifecycle:**
Status Meaning Available Actions
Submitted Workbook uploaded, serial rows populated. Awaiting Ops review Mark Reviewed, Reject Completion
Reviewed Ops has verified the serial data. Ready for acceptance Accept Completion
Accepted As-Built Record and Instance created. Build closed. Terminal state None — read-only
Rejected Serial data had errors. Builder must correct and resubmit None — a new Build Completion is created on resubmission
> **Policy of record:** Reviewed = data check: the uploaded workbook parses, serial rows are complete, and serial rows are well-formed. Accepted = evidence check: Operations verifies serial/label evidence against the returned workbook and confirms the configuration match. Acceptance creates the locked As-Built Record and InductOne Instance. There is no reviewer-not-equal-acceptor constraint. ### Reviewing a Build Completion Open the Build Completion record from the link on the Build form or from the Build Completion list. Verify: - System serial on the completion matches `system_serial` on the parent Build - Component serial rows are populated and plausible for the component types - No unresolved serial warnings remain Click **Mark Reviewed** (Actions menu). Status moves to `Reviewed`. ### Rejecting a Completion If the workbook has errors — wrong serials, missing rows, mismatched components — click **Reject Completion**. You must enter a rejection reason, which is stamped on the record and visible to Operations. After rejection, the CO remains in `Awaiting Completion` status, and the **Upload Builder Completion** button reappears on the Build form. The builder corrects their records and resubmits a new workbook. A new Build Completion record is created on resubmission — the rejected one is retained as a historical record. --- ## Accept Completion — The Atomic Closeout When the Build Completion is `Reviewed`, click **Accept Completion** (Actions menu, styled as a primary button). **Preconditions checked server-side before any writes:** - Build Completion status is `Reviewed` - At least one serial row exists on the completion - The Build link is set on the completion - The Configuration Order link is set on the completion - The parent Build does not already have an As-Built Record (prevents double-acceptance) - The parent Build has `system_serial` set (serial must have been allocated before release) If any precondition fails, the entire operation throws with a specific error message and nothing is written. **If all preconditions pass, the following happens in a single transaction:** 1. `InductOne As-Built Record` is created and immediately locked (`status = Locked`) 2. All serial rows are copied from the Build Completion to the As-Built Record, each row carrying a `source_completion_row` reference for traceability 3. Build Completion status is set to `Accepted` 4. The parent Build is updated: `as_built_record` link set, `completion_status = Accepted`, `build_status = COMPLETED` 5. The Configuration Order is transitioned to `Closed` 6. `InductOne Instance` is created (see below) 7. `frappe.db.commit()` — all writes land together or not at all --- ## The As-Built Record **DocType:** `InductOne As-Built Record` **Navigate to:** As-Built Record list The As-Built Record is the permanent, authoritative CSA record of what was physically built. It is locked the moment it is created and cannot be modified through the UI. **What it contains:** | Field / Section | Content | |---|---| | Build link | The parent `InductOne Build` | | Configuration Order link | The CO that governed this build | | System serial | The `IND-XXXX` serial stenciled on the unit | | Top item and top BOM | The machine variant and BOM at time of build | | Orientation | LH or RH | | Selected options | The configuration options that were selected | | Serial rows | Every component serial captured from the OPS-BLD-F01, with `source_completion_row` traceability | | Locked at / Locked by | Timestamp and user from the acceptance operation | | Status | Always `Locked` — set on creation, never changed | > ⚠️ **The As-Built Record is never edited after creation.** If a serial was captured incorrectly and the error is discovered post-acceptance, the correction path is to add a comment to the As-Built Record documenting the discrepancy, and update the Instance record with the correct information. Contact Operations before making any changes. --- ## The InductOne Instance The `InductOne Instance` is the lifecycle record for a physical machine. It carries the system serial, customer, status, component serial rows, and installation/location context. Location is modeled with `POR Physical Location`, whose tree convention is **Customer → Site → Lane → Cell → Robot**. Operations sets the Instance's **Physical Location** link. The Instance's **Deployment Site** field is read-only and derives from `physical_location.full_path`; it is not a free-text field to maintain by hand. Cell-level linking is the operating convention for installed InductOne units. The underlying link is not hard-filtered to only Cell records, so review should confirm the selected physical location is the intended Cell when an installed unit is being recorded. Field Change records can later update the as-maintained history for an Instance. A Field Change is created by importer/operator workflow, then `accept_field_change` locks it and updates the linked Instance. Field Change Requests do not automatically spawn every Field Change without an explicit import/operator step. --- ## Serial Numbering — The Builder Tranche System System serials (`IND-XXXX`) are issued from **Builder Tranches** — records that govern which serial number range belongs to which builder. **DocType:** `InductOne Builder Tranche` Each tranche record defines: | Field | Description | |---|---| | Builder (Supplier) | The external builder this tranche belongs to | | Status | `Active` or `Retired` | | Tranche Label | Human-readable label (e.g. "2000 series") | | Tranche Start / End | The inclusive integer range for this tranche | | Next Serial | The next integer to be issued. Increments atomically on each allocation | | Serial Prefix | Always `IND` — produces `IND-XXXX` format | **Allocation rules:** - Only `Active` tranches can allocate serials - Each builder has exactly one Active tranche at a time - Ranges cannot overlap with any other tranche, active or retired — overlap is validated on save - `next_serial` must always be within `[tranche_start, tranche_end + 1]` — the `+ 1` state means the tranche is exhausted - Allocation is atomic: the tranche's `next_serial` is incremented in the same operation that stamps `system_serial` on the Build **Retiring a tranche:** Set `status = Retired`. A retired tranche cannot issue new serials but is preserved as a permanent historical record of which serials it issued. Retired ranges still count for overlap validation — the same serial number can never be issued twice, even across builder changes. **Serial allocation cannot be reversed through the UI.** If a serial was allocated in error, contact Operations. Correcting it requires bench console access to clear the field on the Build and decrement `next_serial` on the tranche — both steps together. Skipping either step destroys the audit trail. --- ## Finding Records
What you need Where to find it
As-Built Record for a specific build Open the Build → as_built_record link field
Instance for a specific serial Instance list → search by name (e.g. IND-2042)
Build Completion for a specific build Open the Build → Build Completion link, or Build Completion list filtered by build
All completed builds Build list filtered to COMPLETED
--- ## Common Issues
Accept Completion throws "system_serial missing" The parent Build has no system serial allocated. This should have been done before builder release, but if the build went through an earlier path without it, you must allocate retroactively. Contact Operations. Resolution requires bench console access to stamp the correct serial on the Build and verify it matches what was actually stenciled on the unit.
Accept Completion throws "Build already has an As-Built Record" Acceptance was already completed for this build. The As-Built Record and Instance already exist. Check the Build for the `as_built_record` link and navigate there directly.
Build Completion shows serial warnings after upload The workbook parser could not match some serial rows. Common causes: column headers don't match expected format, serial fields were left blank, or the workbook was a different version of OPS-BLD-F01 than expected. Review the warnings. If the builder left serial fields blank (component not yet assembled), confirm with the builder whether those components are present. Do not accept a completion with genuinely missing serials — reject it and request a corrected workbook.
A serial was captured incorrectly and the build is already Accepted The As-Built Record is locked and cannot be edited. Add a Comment to the As-Built Record documenting the discrepancy (what was captured vs. what the correct serial is, and why). Update the Instance record's notes field with the corrected information. Contact Operations to determine whether a formal correction record is needed for CSA compliance purposes.
--- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · As-Built Records & Instances Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment As-built acceptance closes the loop described in `WI-OPS-BLD-001` and the Builder Completion Serial Workbook (`OPS-BLD-F01`). Audit expectations come from `OPS-CSA-AUD-01`: the physical labels and returned completion evidence must match the ERPNext CSA record. If a mismatch is found after acceptance, do not silently edit history. Use a documented correction path: data-only correction with note, Field Change Order for configuration-changing field updates, or Engineering Change action when the baseline itself needs to change. ## CSA visual reference
As-built and instance lineage
## As-installed register and field-change history ERPNext now treats the `InductOne Instance` list as the live **as-installed / as-maintained register** for InductOne machines. Each Instance is linked to a canonical `POR Physical Location` Cell in the hierarchy: `Customer → Site → Lane → Cell → Robot` For fielded and reference units, the Instance may have `origin = Legacy backfill`, `POC`, or `Internal-Reference` instead of full build provenance. Born-in-system Instances still require the Build, Configuration Order, and As-Built Record links. This lets historical machines and POC units be visible without fabricating records that never existed. Each Instance carries its current physical location, customer/project context, component serial rows, and a rollup of per-machine Field Change history. Field Changes are not edits to the locked As-Built Record. They are separate as-maintained events linked to the Instance, preserving the original as-built truth while showing what changed in the field. ### Field Change lineage on an Instance 1. A field-side issue enters through the FCO intake path. 2. Operations Engineering triages it as Field Change, Deviation, or ECR. 3. Field Change outcomes create `InductOne Field Change Request` records. 4. When implemented on a specific machine, the system creates one `InductOne Field Change` record per affected Instance. 5. Accepted Field Changes become locked as-maintained events and update the Instance rollup (`field_change_count`, `latest_field_change_date`). This is the intended split: **As-Built Record = immutable build truth. Instance = lifecycle/as-maintained truth. Field Change = auditable change event.**

content:
Configured Snapshot Diff
Comparison Tool · What Changed Between Two Builds
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Anyone comparing two configured builds — Engineering reading structural changes, Procurement and Builders reading the buy-list. The tool serves both audiences from the same comparison. > 💡 **Why this tool exists:** A builder who built a machine months ago will lean on muscle memory when they build the next one. Most of that memory is correct and valuable — re-deriving it would waste time and money. But some of it is now *wrong*, because the configuration changed. This tool isolates exactly the parts that must be amended, so prior knowledge gets corrected rather than blindly reused. --- ## What it compares The tool compares two `Configured BOM Snapshot` records directly — the frozen, point-in-time records produced at Stage 3 of the build pipeline. It reads the materialized BOM hierarchy from each snapshot and reports, line by line, exactly what is different. - **Snapshot A** is the *previous / reference* build — the one already built - **Snapshot B** is *this* build — the new one Everything not flagged is unchanged between the two and can be built the same way as before. --- ## Two views, one comparison The same comparison is presented two ways for two audiences. Switch between them with the **View Mode** filter — the report refreshes instantly. | View | Audience | Reads like | Best for | |---|---|---|---| | **Hierarchical** | Engineering | A unified `git diff` down the assembly tree | Seeing *where* in the structure a change lives | | **Flat Procurement** | Procurement / Builder | A rolled-up parts buy-list | Knowing *what to buy differently* | The **Context** filter controls how much is shown: - *Changes only* — prune to just what changed (the hierarchical view keeps the parent path to each change so you can see where it sits) - *Show full tree* / *Show full list* — everything, with changes highlighted --- ## How to use it **Navigate to:** Configured Snapshot Diff report 1. Pick **Snapshot A** — the previous / reference build 2. Pick **Snapshot B** — this build 3. Choose **View Mode** — Hierarchical for engineering, Flat Procurement for the buy-list 4. Choose **Context** — Changes only is the default and the right choice most of the time 5. Read the diff. Summary cards at the top show the counts at a glance 6. Click **Download Diff Workbook** for a color-coded Excel export to send to the builder > 💡 The report page itself carries a legend and an expandable glossary ("What do these mean?") tailored to the active view. If a line is ever unclear, the explanation is one click away above the table. --- ## Reading the diff — what each category means The same color is used on screen and in the downloaded workbook. | Category | Color | Meaning | |---|---|---| | **Added** | Added | A part that was NOT on the previous build and IS on this one. Must be sourced/added this build | | **Removed** | Removed | A part that WAS on the previous build and is NOT on this one. Do not include it this time | | **Revision changed** | Revision | The same part, but now sourced from a different BOM revision than before. Re-verify against the new revision | | **Qty changed** | Qty | The same part, but the total quantity needed is different. Shown as *previous → this build* | | **Moved** | Moved | The same part, now under a different parent assembly in the tree (hierarchical view) | --- ## The things that aren't obvious
Why does the Revision column sometimes show more than one revision (e.g. "003, 005")? Because the part lives in more than one BOM. A part used in two different bills of material — for example a connector used in both the `0921 ELEC` box and a new `0943 ELEC` box — is sourced from each at that BOM's own revision. Showing `005 → 003, 005` means: previously the part came from one BOM at revision 005; now it comes from two BOMs, one at 003 and one at 005. This is normal and correct for shared or common parts. The *Parent* column tells you which assemblies the part sits under, which explains where the multiple revisions come from.
What exactly triggers a "Revision changed" flag? A row is flagged when **its own** sourcing BOM revision changes. This is a deliberate rule: - If a sub-assembly's BOM revision bumps, that sub-assembly is flagged - A parent assembly's revision bump does **not** flag its children unless the child itself actually changed This means an unflagged child is genuinely unchanged — its use, its sourcing, and its procurement are the same as the last build, and prior knowledge still applies to it. The flag is a precise signal, not a blanket cascade. Left-hand / right-hand handedness is never treated as a revision change — LH and RH are genuinely different parts.
What does "Moved" mean, and why does it matter? A moved part is the same part number, in the build both times, but now sits under a different parent assembly in the structure. For *procurement* this often doesn't matter — the buy-list is the same. For *engineering and the builder* it can matter a lot: the part now installs in a different place in the machine. The hierarchical view is where moves are most visible, because they show up as the part appearing under a different branch of the tree.
What does the indentation in the hierarchical view tell me? Rows are indented by their depth in the assembly tree. A change shown indented under a parent assembly means the change happens *inside* that assembly. Parent rows are kept on screen even when they are themselves unchanged, so you can always see where in the structure each change lives — the same way a `git diff` shows the enclosing section around a change.
Why do the two views show different numbers of changes? They count different things. The flat procurement view rolls every part up to a single line and aggregates quantities, so it reflects buy-list impact. The hierarchical view counts changes by structural position, so a part used in three places that changed in all three shows as three changed nodes in the tree but one line in the flat view. Both are correct for their purpose.
--- ## What the diff does NOT do > ⚠️ The diff is a derived, on-demand comparison — it is **not** a record of truth and is not attached to any record. The snapshots themselves are the permanent records. The downloaded workbook is a convenience artifact for sending to a builder; regenerate it any time from the two snapshots. It compares what the snapshots *resolved to*. It does not re-explode live BOMs, does not read current Item master data, and is immune to any BOM or option changes made after the snapshots were frozen. That is the point — each snapshot is a permanent record of its build's configuration, and the diff compares those two frozen states. --- ## For administrators — how it is built > 💡 This section is for System Managers maintaining the tool. Day-to-day users do not need it. The tool lives in the `inductone_tools` app under `snapshot_diff/`. All comparison logic is in version-controlled app code; the report itself is a thin UI Script Report that calls a single whitelisted method. The one point of coupling to how snapshots are stored is isolated in `snapshot_diff/schema.py` — a schema-contract adapter. If the `Configured BOM Snapshot Hierarchy` storage ever changes (a field rename, a new revision field, changed node typing), that one file is updated and the rest of the tool is untouched. The tool fails loudly rather than silently producing a wrong diff if the schema drifts out from under it. A schema version is stamped into every diff for traceability. The revision a part was built against is parsed from the BOM name token (e.g. `BOM-1611 027 0921 ELEC-005` → revision `005`), because there is no dedicated revision column on the hierarchy. If an explicit revision field is added later, the parser is the one place to update. --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · Configured Snapshot Diff Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment Snapshot Diff is the operator-facing evidence mechanism for configuration status accounting. It shows how one as-designed baseline differs from another, supporting Engineering review, procurement clarity, builder communication, and audit traceability. For option-governed changes, the expected evidence chain is: released option → generated snapshot → per-option diff report → released builder package → as-built acceptance. ## CSA visual reference
End-to-end CSA workflow context

content:
BOM Export Package
Builder Handoff Artifact · Configured ZIP Package
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations Engineers generating and releasing build packages. Anyone who needs to understand what the builder receives, what the package contains, and how it connects to the build pipeline. --- ## What a BOM Export Package Is The `BOM Export Package` is the formal, generated artifact that is handed off to the external builder when a build is released. It is a ZIP file produced directly from ERPNext — assembled from the configured BOM, attached schematics, work instructions, balloon report, and the OPS-BLD-F01 serial capture workbook. Before this system existed, builder handoffs were done by emailing BOM spreadsheets and hoping they were current. The BOM Export Package replaces that entirely. Every package is: - Generated from the specific `Configured BOM Snapshot` for that build - Tied to the specific `Configuration Order` and `InductOne Build` - Watermarked with release metadata - Tracked in the CO Document Index - Linked to the build record as `latest_bom_export_package` The builder gets one definitive package with everything they need. There is no ambiguity about what version of the BOM they are building to. --- ## Package Record Location **Navigate to:** BOM Export Package list Or access from the Build form via InductOne → **Generate BOM Export Package**, which opens or creates the package for the current build. --- ## What the Package Contains
File / Artifact Description
Flat Configured BOM (Excel) The resolved flat BOM for this exact build configuration — items included, items excluded, quantities. Derived from the frozen snapshot
Schematics (PDF) PDFs for items in the BOM that have schematics attached. Watermarked with release metadata (build name, release date, revision)
Work Instructions Assembly and build work instructions for the configuration
Balloon Report Ballooned assembly drawing with item callouts keyed to the flat BOM
OPS-BLD-F01 Serial Capture Workbook Pre-populated serial capture workbook with the system serial pre-filled. Component serial fields are blank — the builder fills these in during assembly and returns the completed workbook to Plus One
The ZIP is organized into a folder structure by item — PDFs, STLs, DXFs, and STEP files are placed in per-item subfolders based on the package configuration options (`include_pdf`, `include_stl`, etc.). --- ## Package Settings The BOM Export Package record has configurable generation settings. These are typically set once per build type and should not need adjustment for standard InductOne builds: | Setting | Default | Description | |---|---|---| | `source_mode` | `Configured Build` | How the BOM is sourced — always `Configured Build` for InductOne | | `explosion_mode` | `Follow Explicit Child BOM Links` | How the BOM tree is walked | | `include_pdf` | Yes | Include PDF schematics | | `include_item_attachments` | Yes | Include attachments from Item records | | `include_bom_attachments` | Yes | Include attachments from BOM records | | `include_qty` | Yes | Include quantities in the flat BOM | | `include_stl` | No | STL files (disabled by default) | | `include_step` | No | STEP files (disabled by default) | | `include_dxf` | No | DXF files (disabled by default) | --- ## Creating and Generating a Package ### Step 1 — Open or Create From the Build form, click InductOne → **Generate BOM Export Package**. This button is an **open-or-create** operation: - If a package already exists for this exact combination of Build + Configuration Order + Snapshot, it opens that package - If none exists, it creates a new one pre-populated from the build and navigates to the new record You can also create a package from scratch at the BOM Export Package list, but using the button from the Build form is strongly preferred — it ensures the package is correctly linked to the build, CO, and snapshot. ### Step 2 — Run Generation On the BOM Export Package form, run the generation process. The system: 1. Walks the configured BOM tree using the snapshot's structural effects to include or exclude items and branches 2. Collects attached PDFs, schematics, and work instructions for each included item 3. Watermarks PDFs with release metadata 4. Generates the OPS-BLD-F01 workbook pre-populated with the system serial (if already allocated on the build) 5. Assembles everything into the output ZIP 6. Populates `output_zip` on the package record 7. Registers the package in the CO Document Index > ⚠️ **`output_zip` must be populated before Release to Builder.** The release function checks for the existence of the output ZIP. If the package record exists but generation has not been run, release will fail with a specific error. Do not skip generation. ### Step 3 — Verify Before Release Before clicking Release to Builder, confirm on the package record: - `output_zip` is populated (a file link appears) - The Missing Files Summary (if shown) is empty or contains only expected gaps - The package is linked to the correct build, CO, and snapshot --- ## The OPS-BLD-F01 Workbook The serial capture workbook included in every package is generated from a template and pre-populated with: - Build name and Sales Order reference - System serial (the serial stenciled on the physical unit) — pre-filled when allocated before package generation - Customer project label - Component serial rows — one per tracked serialized component — with serial fields blank for the builder to fill in during assembly The builder fills in component serials during assembly and returns the completed workbook to Plus One when the unit is complete. This returned workbook is what gets uploaded via **Upload Builder Completion** to create the `InductOne Build Completion` record. > 💡 If the system serial was not yet allocated when the package was generated, the OPS-BLD-F01 will have an empty serial field. Allocate the serial and regenerate the package before release so the builder receives the correct workbook with the serial pre-filled. --- ## Package Linking and Deduplication The system prevents duplicate packages through a match on Build + CO + Snapshot. If you click **Generate BOM Export Package** multiple times, you get the same package record each time — not new ones. If you regenerate a CO (e.g. after a re-snapshot), a new CO exists. At that point, **Generate BOM Export Package** will create a new package record linked to the new CO. The old package record is not deleted — it remains as a historical record tied to the old CO. The Build always tracks `latest_bom_export_package`. When the CO is regenerated, this pointer updates to the new package automatically if a package was previously linked. --- ## Package in the CO Document Index When a package is created or regenerated, it is registered as a row in the Configuration Order's Document Index. The document index is the full accounting of all build artifacts associated with the CO — the hierarchy workbook, the export package, the acknowledgement file, and any other documents attached during the lifecycle. This means: if you need to find the package ZIP for an already-released build, open the CO record → Document Index → find the BOM Export Package row → the file link is there. --- ## Common Issues
Release fails with "BOM Export Package has not been generated yet" The `output_zip` field on the package record is empty. The package record was created but generation was not run. **Resolution:** Open the BOM Export Package record from the Build form (click **Generate BOM Export Package** again — it will open the existing record) and run generation on the package form.
Package was generated but the OPS-BLD-F01 has no system serial The serial had not been allocated at the time the package was generated. **Resolution:** Allocate the serial on the Build form (Actions → **Allocate Serial**), then regenerate the package. The new OPS-BLD-F01 will include the serial. Release with the updated package.
Missing Files Summary shows items with no PDFs Items in the configured BOM have no PDF schematics attached in ERPNext, or the attached files are not in a format the package generator can include. **Resolution:** Confirm with Engineering whether the missing items should have schematics. If yes, attach the correct PDF to the Item or BOM record and regenerate the package. If the items legitimately have no schematics, the missing files entry is expected and the package is still valid.
--- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · BOM Export Package Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment The BOM Export Package is the released handoff artifact described by the configuration model and builder serialization work instruction. The package must carry the documents needed for the builder to execute the build without raw ERP access: configuration order, manifest/document index, configured BOM artifacts, balloon callout report, drawings/PDFs, and completion workbook. The builder should not need Item, BOM, Sales Order, or live engineering pages. If a document is required for their work, it belongs in the package or the configuration order document index. ## CSA visual reference

content:
BOM Export Package — Engineering Guide
Standalone Exports · Package Settings · Schematic Review · Quoting

Who this page is for: Engineering users running standalone BOM Export Packages outside the InductOne build pipeline — for schematic reviews, cable schedule exports, and builder quoting.

Two Types of BOM Export

BOM Export Packages are used in two contexts at Plus One Robotics:

Context Who Runs It Purpose
Pipeline export Operations Configured build handoff to builder — tied to a specific Build, Snapshot, and Configuration Order
Standalone export Engineering Schematic review, electrical cable schedule export, or sub-assembly export for quoting — not tied to a build

This page covers standalone exports only. For pipeline exports, see BOM Export Package (Ops) →

Creating a Standalone BOM Export Package

Step 1 — Open a New Package

Navigate to BOM Export Package list → click New.

Step 2 — Set Source Mode

Set Source Mode to Standard BOM. This is the standalone mode — it exports directly from a BOM without requiring a build, snapshot, or configuration order.

💡 Standard BOM vs. Configured Build
Standard BOM exports whatever is in the BOM as-is — no option selections, no configuration logic applied. Use this for Engineering exports. Configured Build is the pipeline mode used by Ops and requires a linked Build record.

Step 3 — Select the BOM

In the BOM field, select the BOM you want to export. This must be an Active, Default, submitted BOM.

For electrical cable schedule exports, select the top-level machine BOM (e.g. BOM-1611 027 0010-004). The cable schedule PDFs are attached to items at depth 1 of that BOM.

For sub-assembly quoting exports, select the specific sub-assembly BOM directly (e.g. a calibration jig BOM).

Package Settings — What Each One Does

Explosion Mode

Setting What It Does When to Use
Follow Explicit Child BOM Links Walks the BOM tree following sub-assembly links, collecting attachments at every level down to the configured depth Standard. Use for most exports
Top Level Only Exports only the direct children of the selected BOM — does not walk into sub-assembly BOMs Electrical cable schedule exports — the cable schedule PDFs live at depth 1 of the top-level BOM. Setting depth 1 with this mode pulls exactly those documents without the full machine BOM tree

Max Depth

Controls how many levels deep the BOM explosion walks. A value of 1 means top-level children only. Leave blank or set high (e.g. 99) for a full-depth export.

For electrical cable schedule exports: set to 1.

For sub-assembly quoting exports: leave blank for full depth, or set to the depth of the sub-assembly.

File Type Toggles

Setting What It Includes
Include PDF PDF drawings, schematics, and documents attached to Item or BOM records. This is the primary output for Engineering exports — enable this
Include STL STL 3D model files attached to items
Include STEP STEP CAD files attached to items
Include DXF DXF 2D drawing files attached to items
Include Item Attachments Collects attachments from the Item record for each BOM line item
Include BOM Attachments Collects attachments from BOM records themselves, not just the items. Cable schedules and assembly-level documents are typically attached here
Include Qty Includes quantity column in the flat BOM spreadsheet output. Useful for quoting — enable this when exporting for a builder quote

Watermark Settings

PDFs in the package are watermarked with release metadata (BOM name, date, revision). This is automatic and cannot be disabled on a per-export basis. For internal review exports, this is expected — the watermark confirms which BOM revision you are reviewing.

Common Export Configurations

Electrical Cable Schedule Export

Setting Value
Source Mode Standard BOM
BOM Top-level machine BOM (e.g. BOM-1611 027 0010-004)
Explosion Mode Top Level Only
Max Depth 1
Include PDF Yes
Include BOM Attachments Yes
All other file types No

The package will include PDFs from the top-level BOM's direct children only. Filter out any items you don't need from the output ZIP.

Sub-Assembly Export for Builder Quoting

Setting Value
Source Mode Standard BOM
BOM The specific sub-assembly BOM (e.g. calibration jig BOM)
Explosion Mode Follow Explicit Child BOM Links
Max Depth Leave blank (full depth)
Include PDF Yes
Include Item Attachments Yes
Include BOM Attachments Yes
Include Qty Yes — builders need quantities for quoting

Running the Export and Downloading the ZIP

  1. With settings configured, click Save on the package record
  2. Click the Generate Package button to run the export
  3. The system walks the BOM tree, collects files, and produces the output ZIP
  4. When complete, the Output ZIP field on the record is populated with a download link
  5. Click the link to download the ZIP

The ZIP is organized into per-item subfolders. Each folder contains the files (PDFs, STLs, etc.) attached to that item or its BOM.

💡 Missing Files Summary
After generation, a Missing Files Summary section may appear listing items that had no attachments of the requested type. Review this — it may indicate that schematics or drawings need to be added to those Item or BOM records in ERPNext before the next export.

Revision History

Date Change Author
2026-06 Initial version Operations
Plus One Robotics · Engineering · BOM Export Package — Engineering Guide Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment Standalone BOM exports are useful engineering/procurement tools, but the released InductOne builder handoff is governed through the build pipeline. When a package is part of a build release, its contents become CSA evidence and must remain reproducible from the configured snapshot and document index. ## CSA visual reference
Builder package composition

content:
Engineering Signoff
BOM · Item · Product Bundle · Configuration Option Approval Gate
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Anyone who sees a signoff banner or release gate on a controlled engineering record and needs to understand what it means and what to do. Engineers with the **Engineering User** role who need to approve or reject records. Operations Engineers blocked on release because of a signoff failure. --- ## What Engineering Signoff Is Engineering Signoff is the ERPNext approval layer for controlled engineering inputs. It records who reviewed a target record, what status that review reached, and whether the target is eligible for release-driven workflows. Signoff applies to four target DocTypes: - **BOM** - **Product Bundle** - **Item** - **InductOne Configuration Option** The model is record-based: a new controlled record gets its own signoff. The code leaves an existing approval record intact when someone later edits an existing target. If a controlled engineering definition changes materially, the defensible path is to create/revise/supersede the record so the new record gets its own review. --- ## Scope Engineering Signoff is checked in two places: 1. On controlled engineering records themselves, where the signoff record preserves approval/rejection history. 2. During InductOne builder release, where the release gate checks the top BOM, top Item, any Product Bundle, and each selected Configuration Option. The release gate does not recursively walk every child BOM in the configured tree and does not compare `target_revision_id` values. It checks the controlled release inputs the build is actually releasing. --- ## The Signoff Lifecycle | Status | Meaning | Release allowed? | |---|---|---| | `Pending` | Review has been requested and no approval/rejection has been recorded yet. | No | | `Approved` | An authorized approver accepted the target record for controlled use. | Yes | | `Rejected` | The target was reviewed and rejected. It must be corrected through the appropriate record/revision path. | No | | `Superseded` | This signoff was replaced by a later controlled record/version and remains for audit history. | No for new release decisions | --- ## How Signoff Records Are Created For **BOM**, **Item**, and **Product Bundle**, a Pending signoff is created on `after_insert` of the new record. This keeps first-release review tied to the actual record that was created. For **InductOne Configuration Option**, signoff is requested manually while the option is still `Draft`. The option must have `mapping_status = Complete` before it can be approved. Approval of the signoff is the controlled release action: it moves the option from `Draft` to `Released` and locks it against direct edit. --- ## What the Banner Shows On every BOM and Product Bundle form, a banner appears near the top showing the current signoff status. The banner is visible to all users regardless of role.
✓ Engineering Signoff — Approved
Approved by Shaun Edwards · 2026-05-14 · "Reviewed rev B items table, all correct."
⏳ Engineering Signoff — Pending Review
Requested by Michael King · 2026-05-15 · Awaiting Engineering review
✗ Engineering Signoff — Rejected
Rejected by Jason Minica · 2026-05-12 · "Qty on row 14 is incorrect — should be 2, not 4."
The banner shows: status, reviewer name, timestamp, and notes if any were entered. This gives Ops full visibility into the approval state without needing to open the signoff record. --- ## Approving or Rejecting a Signoff **Who can approve or reject:** Engineering User, InductOne Process Architect, or System Manager. Approving records the approver, timestamp, notes, and Approved status. Rejecting records the reviewer, timestamp, notes, and Rejected status. There is no separate undo action; if a released engineering definition needs to change, create the appropriate new revision/successor and review that record. --- ## Configuration Option Release Signoff Configuration Options have an extra release behavior because they control what loads into build selections: 1. Option is created or revised in `Draft`. 2. Mapping rows are completed and `mapping_status` is set to `Complete`. 3. A signoff is requested for the option. 4. Approval by Engineering User, InductOne Process Architect, or System Manager promotes the option to `Released` and locks it. 5. Future changes use Supersede, which creates a new Draft successor and keeps the old option auditable. --- ## How Release Gating Works When **Prepare Builder Handoff** or **Release to Builder** runs, the builder-release gate checks the controlled records that define the released build: - Top BOM - Top Item - Product Bundle, when the build uses one - Each selected InductOne Configuration Option Each required target must have a current Approved Engineering Signoff. The gate does not recursively require every child BOM in the configured tree and does not require a revision-id match on child records. If a gate fails, the readiness output names the blocker so Operations can route it to Engineering User, InductOne Process Architect, or System Manager for review. --- ## Engineering Signoff Workspace The Engineering workspace in ERPNext has a dedicated Signoff queue showing all Pending signoffs currently awaiting review. Engineers should check this queue regularly, especially before a planned builder release. **Direct links:** - Pending Signoffs (current) - All Signoff Records (history) --- ## Common Scenarios **A new BOM, Item, or Product Bundle exists but release is blocked.** Open the linked Engineering Signoff and have an authorized approver review it. New records are expected to start Pending. **A Configuration Option is still Draft.** Complete its mappings, set `mapping_status = Complete`, request Engineering Signoff, and approve it through the signoff gate. Draft options do not load into build selections. **A released option needs to change.** Use Supersede to create a Draft successor. Do not directly edit the released option. **A build release names a blocker.** Treat the readiness output as the source of truth. It is checking top BOM, top Item, Product Bundle when present, and selected Configuration Options. --- ## Revision History | Date | Change | Author | |---|---|---| | 2026-06 | Initial version | Operations | ---
Plus One Robotics · Manufacturing & Configuration / CSA · Engineering Signoff Robots work. People rule.
## CSA source alignment Engineering Signoff is the approval gate that prevents uncontrolled records from entering the build pipeline. It applies to BOMs, Product Bundles, and governed Configuration Options. Approval should mean the record is ready to be used by downstream Operations without hidden manual interpretation. Configuration Option signoff is especially important: Draft options can exist for ideation, but only the signoff gate promotes an option into the Released catalog that builds may load. ## CSA visual reference
Configuration option status gate

content:
Field Change (FCO) Register
Request Intake · Per-Instance Field Changes · SUP-FCO-R01 Export
> 📋 **Who this page is for:** Operations Engineering, InductOne Managers, Process Architects, and auditors who need to understand the FCO register model and how field changes become as-maintained Instance history. ## Model The FCO register is implemented as two ERPNext record types: | Record | Purpose | |---|---| | `InductOne Field Change Request` | Intake, triage, disposition, assignment, and register row. This is the operating ledger for SUP-FCO-R01. | | `InductOne Field Change` | Per-Instance as-maintained event created when an approved/implemented request affects a specific machine. Locked Field Changes are locked. | A Request may exist without a Field Change when the item is pending, rejected, cancelled, fleet/design-level only, or waiting on a real Instance serial. A Field Change is created only when there is an implemented per-machine event. ## Intake and disposition flow `JotForm intake → ERPNext import → Operations Engineering triage → Field Change / Deviation / ECR disposition → implementation → as-maintained Instance update → closed register row` - Field-side JotForm submissions are imported into `InductOne Field Change Request` records. - The importer deduplicates by `intake_ref` / FCO number. - Triage outcome remains explicit: Field Change, Deviation, or ECR. - Repeated/site-wide changes reference the Engineering Change process (`ECR → ECO → ECN`) instead of pretending ERPNext owns design release. ## Assignment rule The assigned Instance is tracked but changeable. Backfilled assignments may be low-confidence. Reassignment requires an assignment-change reason and the DocType has change tracking enabled, so the Version log records who changed the assignment and why. ## SUP-FCO-R01 `SUP-FCO-R01` is a generated report/export from ERPNext, not a parallel hand-maintained spreadsheet. The controlled template is `SUP-FCO-R01_FCORegister-v2-0.xlsx`; the operating instance location is to be designated after release. Use the ERPNext report `SUP-FCO-R01 Field Change Register` to export the current 18-column register view. ## Pending / organic handling Known pending additions remain documented instead of fabricated: - Worldport Primary-3 cells `WP-C-3-2` and `WP-C-3-3` are seeded as locations, but their real IND serials are pending. - FCO-2026-019 remains Request-only until the true Instance serials are designated. - Organic/untracked cases remain Request-only until the owner designates or creates a real Instance.
